summary: Justin Brennan conducts a mentorship Q&A covering deal sourcing challenges, underwriting techniques, and capital raising strategies. Key discussions include analyzing off-market deals, negotiating broker fees, understanding competitor capital structures, and setting realistic price-per-unit targets for first-time operators. A live example features a 33-unit Missouri deal needing $80-100K in final capital to close.
takeaways: Submit 15-20 LOIs before expecting one to land; don't get attached to any single deal
Earnest money should be 1-2% due within 72 hours of executed contract, not 5% over 20 days
Expense ratios: 1970-1990s properties target 40-50%, post-1990 can achieve 30s; verify brokers aren't hiding expenses in capex
Interest rate should not exceed current cap rate; for example, 6.5% cap rate cannot support 7.35% debt without significant down payment
Price-per-unit sweet spot is $50,000-$150,000 to keep capital raises bitesizable ($300K-$700K range)
Call brokers on lost deals asking: 'What's their capital structure, down payment %, debt rate, and terms?' to understand competitive positioning

For co-GP partnerships, allocate percentages by responsibility: deal finder 10%, due diligence 10%, boots-on-ground 10%, asset manager 15%, loan signer 20%, earnest money/risk capital 10%, capital raiser 30%

skill_candidates.change: Verify property tax and insurance numbers match regional benchmarks before accepting broker financials
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin catches Leonardo's low expense ratio, recommends checking these 'big four' components (taxes, insurance, maintenance, repairs) and verifying capex done to HVAC/electrical/plumbing on older properties

skill_candidates.change: After losing a deal, call broker to understand winner's capital terms rather than accept silence
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin coaches Tony and Ryan to ask Jason: 'What % down and what debt rate did they get?' This reveals competitive advantage and builds rapport for future deals

skill_candidates.change: Submit low offers immediately as 'exercise' to meet brokers, even if not ready to close until September
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin tells Cristiano: 'Throw offers out there. I don't care if they're low offers... it's to go through that initial process... to actually meet brokers and talk to brokers'

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Deal Momentum_06-10-25
